title: MEP-52 research bundle: Mochi to TypeScript / JavaScript transpiler

files.label: Language surface: Mochi onto TypeScript 5.6 / ES2024

Design philosophy: why TypeScript, why ES2024 floor, why tsc --strict + --noUncheckedIndexedAccess dual gates, why AsyncIterableQueue + AbortController, why npm canonical with four-runtime conditional exports
MEP-52 research note 03, Prior art and source-to-TypeScript/JavaScript tooling
MEP-52 research note 04, The mochi_runtime npm package
Codegen design: aotir to TypeScript AST via Mochi-side syntax tree printer, monomorphisation, closure conversion, match-to-switch-tag, source maps
Type lowering: per-Mochi-type details onto TypeScript 5.6
MEP-52 research note 07, Runtime portability: Node x Deno x Bun x Browser
MEP-52 research note 08, Dataset and query pipeline (TS / JS lowering)
Agents and streams: AsyncIterableQueue + AbortController, AsyncIterable streams, cold/hot patterns, structured concurrency without TaskGroup
Build system: package.json + tsc + npm publish, four-runtime conditional exports, JSR for Deno, esbuild for browser, npm Trusted Publishing (Sigstore + provenance)

Testing gates: per-phase Go test gates, four-runtime matrix (Node 22 / Deno 2 / Bun 1.1 / browser via Playwright), tsc --strict + --noUncheckedIndexedAccess + --exactOptionalPropertyTypes, eslint + prettier fixed-point, npm pack + install + execute, ~400-fixture corpus by Phase 18

Risks and alternatives: 15 risks + 6 rejected alternatives (Babel, esbuild-only, JSDoc-only, Webpack, Rollup, RxJS) + 4 future candidates (WASM via WASI, ts-blank-space for zero-build, Bun-native compile, Cloudflare Workers)

title: MEP-55 research bundle: Mochi to PHP 8.4 transpiler

files.label: Language surface: Mochi features mapped to PHP 8.4 lowering obligations
Design philosophy: why PHP 8.4, why strict_types, why sync wrappers, why final readonly, why DJB2/GMP, why Phar
Prior art: source-to-PHP tools, PHP version history, and why aotir→ptree→emit
Codegen design: aotir → ptree → emit pipeline
Type lowering: per-Mochi-type PHP 8.4 lowering rules
PHP target portability: CI matrix, phar.readonly, FrankenPHP, RoadRunner, Packagist
Dataset pipeline: query DSL lowering and Datalog compile-time evaluation
Agents and streams: Phase 9 agents, Phase 10 streams, Phase 11 async coloring
Build system: Driver.Build pipeline, resolvePhp, cacheKey, Phase 17 packaging, CI workflow
Testing gates: two-tier strategy, fragment tests, PHP execution tests, DJB2, Phar, reproducibility
Risks and alternatives: scheduling, PHP_INT_MAX overflow, Psalm 5 vs 6, abstract readonly, phar.readonly, amphp removed, live LLM deferred, PHP 8.5

title: MEP-56 research bundle: Mochi to Ruby transpiler
MEP-57 research bundle: Mochi module and package system

files.label: Language surface: package imports, workspaces, capabilities
Design philosophy: the six load-bearing decisions
Prior art: registries and package managers (2024-2026 survey)
Manifest format: mochi.toml schema deep dive
Lockfile format: mochi.lock canonical TOML serialisation
Content-addressed object store: BLAKE3 plus SHA-256
Trusted publishing: Sigstore plus OIDC end-to-end
Capability model: closed set, manifest-declared, target-enforced
Polyglot fan-out: one source, eight ecosystem artifacts
